answers: Hydropower generates electricity by using the energy of flowing or falling water to spin turbines. It's one of the oldest and most reliable renewable energy sources.

Hydroelectric power plants use dams or river currents to generate electricity. It provides about 16% of the world's electricity and is highly reliable and efficient.
Hydropower is a renewable energy source that converts the kinetic energy of water into electricity. It produces no direct emissions and can provide both baseload and peak power.
